commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed for contributor/live proof and canonical selection between overlapping PRs; there is no narrow code repair to queue from this review.; Cleared: The diff changes auth-error classification, reply ordering, and tests only; it does not add dependencies, workflows, secret storage, or code-execution surfaces. (sha=9c1895e882fcc706a809f64932561db8e62beb60)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Claude CLI expired-OAuth 401 classification, moves OAuth re-auth handling ahead of generic provider-auth replies, special-cases the Anthropic CLI login command, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +45, Tests +52. Total +97 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main misses the reported Claude CLI 401 text in the OAuth refresh classifier and can route a typed auth 401 through generic provider-auth copy first. I did not run a live expired-token Claude CLI session.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed for contributor/live proof and canonical selection between overlapping PRs; there is no narrow code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(claude-cli): surface re-auth hint when subprocess OAuth token expires
target.body:
Fixes #97553

## What Problem This Solves

When `claude-cli/*` is the active model and Claude CLI's stored OAuth token has expired, the subprocess can fail with:

```text
Failed to authenticate. API Error: 401 Invalid authentication credentials
```

Before this fix, that error was wrapped as a provider auth `FailoverError` and surfaced as generic provider-auth copy:

```text
⚠️ Authentication failed (provider returned HTTP 401). Your provider token may have expired — try the request again in a moment. If the failure persists, re-authenticate this provider.
```

The user did not get the actionable Claude CLI re-login command.

## Root Cause

Two branches intercepted the failure before the targeted OAuth-refresh handling could run:

- `isOAuthRefreshFailureMessage` did not recognize the Claude CLI subprocess 401 shape.
- `classifyProviderRequestError` handled the typed 401 `FailoverError` first, so the generic auth response won before the OAuth-refresh classifier could produce the targeted re-auth hint.

## Fix

- Recognize the Claude CLI subprocess 401 message as an OAuth refresh failure for `claude-cli`.
- Route the auto-reply/fallback failure path through OAuth-refresh classification before the generic provider-auth classifier.
- Emit the actual CLI re-login command for this provider shape:

```text
openclaw models auth login --provider anthropic --method cli
```

## Evidence

Focused local validation after the assertion follow-up:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -- --run

Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  220 passed (220)
```

Focused lint validation after the follow-up:

```text
oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts

(no findings)
```

New/updated tests cover:

- Claude CLI subprocess 401 OAuth expiry is classified as a provider refresh failure.
- Non-Claude-CLI 401s remain negative controls and continue through existing generic handling.
- The full `runAgentTurnWithFallback` path with a real `FailoverError { reason: "auth", status: 401, provider: "claude-cli" }` surfaces the targeted re-auth hint using `openclaw models auth login --provider anthropic --method cli` instead of generic provider-auth text.

## Security & Privacy

No secrets, credentials, or user data are read or written. The change only classifies an existing error shape and changes the recovery hint shown to the user.

## Compatibility

Existing OAuth refresh handling for other providers is unchanged. The Claude CLI guard requires the `claude-cli` provider context plus the subprocess 401 phrase, so unrelated provider 401 failures continue to use existing generic provider-auth handling.

_AI-assisted (Claude Sonnet 4.6 + manual review and test validation)_
target.author: alix-007
